edges.mechanism: Bioactive GA binds the GID1 receptor, enabling GID1 to interact with DELLA proteins

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1s bind to bioactive GAs with high affinity, but not to inactive GA derivatives

edges.mechanism: GID1-GA-DELLA complex recruits SCF-SLY1 E3 ligase that polyubiquitinates DELLA for 26S proteasome degradation

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA de-represses its signaling pathway by binding to GID1s, which induce degradation of GA signaling repressors (DELLAs) via an ubiquitin-proteasome pathway
evidence.claim: GID1 promotes DELLA degradation upon GA binding

edges.mechanism: GA promotes DELLA degradation via GID1 receptor and SCF-SLY1 ubiquitination

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, Horrer D, Zhang TQ, Hao YH, Feng YQ, Wang S, Schmid M, Wang JW
evidence.evidence_sentence: GAs regulate gene expression by promoting the degradation of the transcriptional regulator DELLA proteins

edges.mechanism: DELLA directly activates GA biosynthesis genes as homeostatic feedback

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: Expression of all 14 putative DELLA target genes are DELLA-induced and GA-repressed; DELLA targets include GA20ox2, GA3ox1 (biosynthesis), GID1a, GID1b (receptors)
evidence.claim: DELLA upregulates GA biosynthesis enzymes (negative feedback)

edges.mechanism: GA catabolism genes are upregulated by GA (i.e., downregulated when DELLA accumulates)

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: The GA catabolism genes GA2ox1 and GA2ox2 are up-regulated by GA treatment
evidence.claim: DELLA represses GA2ox (inverse of GA effect)

edges.mechanism: GA20ox catalyzes the penultimate step in bioactive GA production

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of GA20ox: early flowering and increased stem growth phenotypes

edges.mechanism: GA2ox catabolizes bioactive GA to inactive forms

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tai-ping Sun
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of GA2ox: late flowering and dwarf phenotypes

edges.mechanism: DELLA physically binds SPL transcription factors via C-terminal domain, preventing their activation of target genes

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, Horrer D, Zhang TQ, Hao YH, Feng YQ, Wang S, Schmid M, Wang JW
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA directly binds to miR156-targeted SPL transcription factors; The C-terminal domain of SPL9 is responsible for interaction with RGA

edges.mechanism: DELLA physically binds SPL15 suppressing its transcriptional activity

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, Horrer D, Zhang TQ, Hao YH, Feng YQ, Wang S, Schmid M, Wang JW
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA directly binds to miR156-targeted SPL transcription factors (SPL3, SPL4, SPL5, SPL9, SPL15)

edges.mechanism: SPL9 directly binds the SOC1 promoter to activate transcription; DELLA-SPL interaction blocks this

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, Horrer D, Zhang TQ, Hao YH, Feng YQ, Wang S, Schmid M, Wang JW
evidence.evidence_sentence: ProSPL9:rSPL9 plants show elevated SOC1 and FUL expression; DELLA-SPL interaction interferes with SPL transcriptional activation of miR172 and MADS box genes

edges.mechanism: SPL9 activates FUL transcription; DELLA interaction represses this activation

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, Horrer D, Zhang TQ, Hao YH, Feng YQ, Wang S, Schmid M, Wang JW
evidence.evidence_sentence: ProSPL9:rSPL9 plants show elevated SOC1 and FUL expression

edges.mechanism: SPL9/SPL15 transcription factors promote LFY expression during floral transition

evidence.title: Beyond the Genetic Pathways, Flowering Regulation Complexity in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Quiroz S, Yustis JC, Chavez-Hernandez EC, Martinez T, Sanchez MP, Garay-Arroyo A, Alvarez-Buylla ER, Garcia-Ponce B
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL9/10/15 targets: MIR172, FUL, LFY, AP1 (direct promoter binding)

edges.mechanism: DELLA acts as coactivator of GAF1; GA-induced DELLA degradation converts GAF1 complex from activator to repressor

evidence.title: DELLA degradation by gibberellin promotes flowering via GAF1-TPR-dependent repression of floral repressors in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Fukazawa J, Mori K, Ando H, Mori R, Kanno Y, Seo M, Takahashi Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLAs are coactivators of the transcription factor GAI ASSOCIATED FACTOR 1 (GAF1); GAs convert the GAF1 complex from a transcriptional activator to a repressor

edges.mechanism: GAF1-TPR corepressor complex represses floral repressor ELF3

evidence.title: DELLA degradation by gibberellin promotes flowering via GAF1-TPR-dependent repression of floral repressors in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Fukazawa J, Mori K, Ando H, Mori R, Kanno Y, Seo M, Takahashi Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: GAF1 forms a transcriptional repressor complex and promotes FT and SOC1 through the repression of four flowering repressor genes (ELF3, SVP, TEM1, TEM2)
evidence.claim: GAF1 represses ELF3 via TPR corepressor

edges.mechanism: GAF1-TPR corepressor binds SVP promoter to repress transcription

evidence.title: DELLA degradation by gibberellin promotes flowering via GAF1-TPR-dependent repression of floral repressors in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Fukazawa J, Mori K, Ando H, Mori R, Kanno Y, Seo M, Takahashi Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: GAF1 forms a transcriptional repressor complex and promotes FT and SOC1 through the repression of four flowering repressor genes

edges.mechanism: TPR is the corepressor that converts GAF1 into an active repressor upon DELLA loss

evidence.title: DELLA degradation by gibberellin promotes flowering via GAF1-TPR-dependent repression of floral repressors in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Fukazawa J, Mori K, Ando H, Mori R, Kanno Y, Seo M, Takahashi Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: The DELLA-GAF1 complex activated but repressed by the TPR4-GAF1 complex in transient assays
evidence.claim: TPR is corepressor partner of GAF1

edges.mechanism: SVP directly represses GA20ox2 transcription at the shoot apex, reducing GA biosynthesis

evidence.title: SHORT VEGETATIVE PHASE reduces gibberellin biosynthesis at the Arabidopsis shoot apex to regulate the floral transition
evidence.authors: Andres F, Porri A, Torti S, Mateos J, Romera-Branchat M, Garcia-Martinez JL, Fornara F, Gregis V, Kater MM, Coupland G
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP reduces the transcription of GA20ox2 via downregulation at SAM

edges.mechanism: SOC1 directly binds SVP regulatory regions to repress it during flowering

evidence.title: SHORT VEGETATIVE PHASE reduces gibberellin biosynthesis at the Arabidopsis shoot apex to regulate the floral transition
evidence.authors: Andres F, Porri A, Torti S, Mateos J, Romera-Branchat M, Garcia-Martinez JL, Fornara F, Gregis V, Kater MM, Coupland G
evidence.evidence_sentence: SOC1 binds directly to SVP to contribute to its repression during flowering

edges.mechanism: DELLA physically interacts with BRM chromatin remodeler to recruit it

evidence.title: Gibberellin signaling modulates flowering via the DELLA-BRAHMA-NF-YC module in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Zhang Y, Liu Z, Wang J, Chen Y, Bi Y, He Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLAs physically interact with BRM demonstrated via yeast 2-hybrid, pull-down, and co-IP assays

edges.mechanism: BRM represses SOC1 by reducing H3K4me3 deposition at the SOC1 locus

evidence.title: Gibberellin signaling modulates flowering via the DELLA-BRAHMA-NF-YC module in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Zhang Y, Liu Z, Wang J, Chen Y, Bi Y, He Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRM negatively regulates flowering repressing SOC1 with decreased H3K4me3 deposition

edges.mechanism: NF-YC transcription factors activate SOC1, antagonizing BRM inhibition

evidence.title: Gibberellin signaling modulates flowering via the DELLA-BRAHMA-NF-YC module in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Zhang Y, Liu Z, Wang J, Chen Y, Bi Y, He Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: NF-YCs activate SOC1 antagonistically opposing BRM inhibition

edges.mechanism: DELLA enhances BRM-NF-YC interaction

evidence.title: Gibberellin signaling modulates flowering via the DELLA-BRAHMA-NF-YC module in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Zhang Y, Liu Z, Wang J, Chen Y, Bi Y, He Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins enhance the interaction of BRM with NF-YCs (yeast 3-hybrid, split-LUC assays)

edges.mechanism: GA4 activates LFY transcription through a GA-responsive element in the LFY promoter (GA-myb binding site)

evidence.title: GA4 Is the Active Gibberellin in the Regulation of LEAFY Transcription and Arabidopsis Floral Initiation
evidence.authors: Eriksson S, Bohlenius H, Moritz T, Nilsson O
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA effect on LFY is mediated through a GA-response site in the LFY promoter with similarities to a GA-myb binding site

edges.mechanism: DELLA inhibits LFY transcription, counteracting GA induction

evidence.title: GA4 Is the Active Gibberellin in the Regulation of LEAFY Transcription and Arabidopsis Floral Initiation
evidence.authors: Eriksson S, Bohlenius H, Moritz T, Nilsson O
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA-deficient ga1-3 mutants fail to flower when grown under short days, with inability to upregulate LFY

edges.mechanism: DELLA represses SOC1 via multiple routes (SPL binding, GAF1 coactivation of repressors, BRM interaction)

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SOC1 integrates the GA-mediated flowering-time signal

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SOC1 expression is up-regulated under long-day conditions by CONSTANS, in a process that to a large extent depends on the presence of the FT protein

edges.mechanism: FLC directly binds SOC1 promoter CArG boxes to repress transcription

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLC represses the expression of SOC1 via direct binding to the SOC1 promoter region

edges.mechanism: SVP binds SOC1 regulatory sequences directly, often as a complex with FLC

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP binds SOC1 regulatory sequences as well

edges.mechanism: AGL24 acts as direct inducer of SOC1; heterodimerizes with SOC1 protein to enter nucleus

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: AGL24 acts as a direct inducer of SOC1 and flowering

edges.mechanism: SOC1 directly binds LFY regulatory sequences (ChIP-seq) to activate transcription

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: peak of SOC1 expression just prior to the floral transition precedes LFY up-regulation explained by direct binding of SOC1 to LFY regulatory sequences

edges.mechanism: SOC1 directly activates SEP3 (one of the top ChIP-seq targets)

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: ChIP-seq identified SEP3 as one of the most strongly enriched SOC1 targets

edges.mechanism: SOC1 directly promotes AGL24 mRNA expression (positive feedback loop)

evidence.title: AGL24 acts in concert with SOC1 and FUL during Arabidopsis floral transition
evidence.authors: Torti S, Fornara F
evidence.evidence_sentence: SOC1 can directly promote AGL24 mRNA expression

edges.mechanism: SOC1 binds its own locus strongly, indicating autoregulatory negative feedback

evidence.title: Characterization of SOC1's Central Role in Flowering by the Identification of Its Upstream and Downstream Regulators
evidence.authors: Immink RGH, Pose D, Ferrario S, Ott F, Kaufmann K, Valentim FL, de Folter S, van der Wal F, van Dijk AD, Schmid M, Angenent GC
evidence.evidence_sentence: strongest binding of SOC1 to its own locus indicative of strong feedback regulation of SOC1 by its own gene product

edges.mechanism: AGL24 (with SOC1) activates LFY transcription; 35S::LFY rescues agl24 late flowering

evidence.evidence_sentence: AGL24 regulates transcriptional induction of LEAFY; 35S:LFY almost completely rescues agl24 late-flowering phenotype

evidence.title: AGAMOUS-LIKE 24, a dosage-dependent mediator of the flowering signals
evidence.authors: Yu H, Xu Y, Tan EL, Kumar PP
evidence.evidence_sentence: Vernalization increases AGL24 expression

edges.mechanism: GA dramatically elevates AGL24 transcript levels

evidence.title: AGAMOUS-LIKE 24, a dosage-dependent mediator of the flowering signals
evidence.authors: Yu H, Xu Y, Tan EL, Kumar PP
evidence.evidence_sentence: Gibberellic acid (GA) dramatically elevates AGL24 levels

evidence.title: The MADS-Domain Factors AGAMOUS-LIKE15 and AGAMOUS-LIKE18, along with SHORT VEGETATIVE PHASE and AGAMOUS-LIKE24, Are Necessary to Block Floral Gene Expression during the Vegetative Phase

evidence.title: The MADS-Domain Factors AGAMOUS-LIKE15 and AGAMOUS-LIKE18, along with SHORT VEGETATIVE PHASE and AGAMOUS-LIKE24, Are Necessary to Block Floral Gene Expression during the Vegetative Phase

evidence.title: A Quantitative and Dynamic Model of the Arabidopsis Flowering Time Gene Regulatory Network
evidence.authors: Leal Valentim F, van Mourik S, Pose D, Kim MC, Schmid M, van Ham RCHJ, Busscher M, Sanchez-Perez GF, Molenaar J, Angenent GC, Immink RGH, van Dijk ADJ

evidence.authors: Leal Valentim F, van Mourik S, Pose D, Kim MC, Schmid M, van Ham RCHJ, Busscher M, Sanchez-Perez GF, Molenaar J, Angenent GC, Immink RGH, van Dijk ADJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: SVP and FLC repress the transcription of FT in leaves

edges.mechanism: LFY directly activates AP1 transcription; onset of AP1 expression is delayed in lfy mutants

evidence.title: Interactions among APETALA1, LEAFY, and TERMINAL FLOWER1 specify meristem fate

evidence.title: Interactions among APETALA1, LEAFY, and TERMINAL FLOWER1 specify meristem fate
evidence.authors: Liljegren SJ, Gustafson-Brown C, Pinyopich A, Ditta GS, Yanofsky MF
evidence.evidence_sentence: LFY is expressed prematurely in the converted floral meristems of plants constitutively expressing AP1

evidence.authors: Zhu Y, Klasfeld S, Jeong CW, Jin R, Goto K, Yamaguchi N, Wagner D
evidence.evidence_sentence: LFY: Repression via exonic G-box and C-boxes; reporters with mutated bZIP sites show ectopic expression in apex

evidence.authors: Liljegren SJ, Gustafson-Brown C, Pinyopich A, Ditta GS, Yanofsky MF
evidence.evidence_sentence: TFL1 expression is downregulated in plants constitutively expressing AP1

edges.mechanism: LFY binds cis-regulatory elements ~2.8 kb downstream of TFL1 promoter

evidence.authors: Liljegren SJ, Gustafson-Brown C, Pinyopich A, Ditta GS, Yanofsky MF

evidence.title: Arabidopsis TERMINAL FLOWER1 Is Involved in the Regulation of Flowering Time and Inflorescence Development through Transcriptional Repression

evidence.title: Orchestration of the Floral Transition and Floral Development in Arabidopsis by the Bifunctional Transcription Factor APETALA2
evidence.authors: Yant L, Mathieu J, Dinh TT, Ott F, Lanz C, Wollmann H, Chen X, Schmid M
evidence.evidence_sentence: ap2 Accelerated flowering: 25.3 +/- 1.4 leaves (SD) vs. 47.8 +/- 2.1 in wild-type

evidence.title: NO FLOWERING IN SHORT DAY (NFL) is a bHLH transcription factor that promotes flowering specifically under short-day conditions in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sharma N, Xin R, Kim D-H, Sung S, Lange T, Huq E
evidence.evidence_sentence: nfl mutant: Fails to flower under short-day (8h:16h light:dark) conditions despite producing ~100 rosette leaves
evidence.claim: nfl fails to flower in SD

evidence.title: NO FLOWERING IN SHORT DAY (NFL) is a bHLH transcription factor that promotes flowering specifically under short-day conditions in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sharma N, Xin R, Kim D-H, Sung S, Lange T, Huq E
evidence.evidence_sentence: exogenous application of GA4 rescues the late-flowering phenotype (of nfl mutant)

evidence.title: Orchestration of the Floral Transition and Floral Development in Arabidopsis by the Bifunctional Transcription Factor APETALA2
evidence.authors: Yant L, Mathieu J, Dinh TT, Ott F, Lanz C, Wollmann H, Chen X, Schmid M
evidence.evidence_sentence: soc1-2 ap2-12 double mutant flowered only 1 leaf later than wild-type (ap2 suppresses soc1 lateness)

evidence.title: Orchestration of the Floral Transition and Floral Development in Arabidopsis by the Bifunctional Transcription Factor APETALA2
evidence.authors: Yant L, Mathieu J, Dinh TT, Ott F, Lanz C, Wollmann H, Chen X, Schmid M
evidence.evidence_sentence: ap2-12 suppressed late-flowering mutations (soc1-2, ft-10, co-9)
evidence.claim: ap2 partially suppresses ft late flowering

evidence.title: A Quantitative and Dynamic Model of the Arabidopsis Flowering Time Gene Regulatory Network
evidence.authors: Leal Valentim F, van Mourik S, Pose D, Kim MC, Schmid M, van Ham RCHJ, Busscher M, Sanchez-Perez GF, Molenaar J, Angenent GC, Immink RGH, van Dijk ADJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: soc1/agl24 double mutant: Most severe late-flowering phenotype with additive effects

evidence.title: AGL24 acts in concert with SOC1 and FUL during Arabidopsis floral transition
evidence.authors: Torti S, Fornara F
evidence.evidence_sentence: soc1 ful agl24 triple mutant: Mutations in agl24 can delay flowering by around 9 rosette leaves
evidence.claim: soc1 ful agl24 triple mutant severely delays flowering

evidence.title: Gibberellin signaling modulates flowering via the DELLA-BRAHMA-NF-YC module in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Zhang Y, Liu Z, Wang J, Chen Y, Bi Y, He Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: brm-3 ga1 shows rescue toward earlier flowering (brm suppresses ga1 late flowering)

evidence.title: GA4 Is the Active Gibberellin in the Regulation of LEAFY Transcription and Arabidopsis Floral Initiation

evidence.evidence_sentence: LFY expression from a constitutive promoter induces flowering in a ga1-3 mutant background

evidence.title: AGAMOUS-LIKE 24, a dosage-dependent mediator of the flowering signals
